summary: Outbox Consumer Admin API testing complete. All 25 tests pass (1 skipped - no dead-lettered events to test retry). All 8 admin endpoints verified working. Celery + Redis + Outbox system fully operational.
seed_data_creation: None (using existing outbox_events data - 11 events already dispatched)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Outbox Consumer system is fully operational. All 11 existing outbox_events have status='dispatched'. To test with pending events, create new events or reset existing events to pending status. Celery worker and beat are running via supervisor. Redis is on localhost:6379 (DB 1 for broker).
features_verified: GET /api/admin/outbox/health — Returns status=healthy, health_score=100, redis_status=connected, 10 event types registered
GET /api/admin/outbox/stats — Returns status_counts with dispatched=11, pending=0, dead_lettered=0
GET /api/admin/outbox/pending — Returns empty list (all events already dispatched)
GET /api/admin/outbox/failed — Returns empty list (no dead-lettered events)
GET /api/admin/outbox/dispatch-table — Returns all 10 event types with 35 total handlers (5 consumer types)
POST /api/admin/outbox/trigger — Successfully triggers manual poll (events_claimed=0 since all dispatched)
POST /api/admin/outbox/retry/{event_id} — Returns not_found for non-existent event
GET /api/admin/outbox/consumer-log — Returns 10 recent consumer processing logs with handler results
Authentication required — All endpoints return 401 without token
Five consumer types verified: send_booking_notification, send_booking_email, update_billing_projection, update_reporting_projection, dispatch_webhook

Event dispatch table completeness — All 10 event types covered: booking.confirmed, booking.cancelled, booking.quoted, booking.completed, booking.amended, booking.refunded, payment.completed, payment.failed, booking.ticketed, booking.vouchered
Idempotency verified — Double trigger does not create duplicate results (consumers check outbox_consumer_results)
Celery worker running on queues: default, notification_queue, reports
Celery beat running with 5-second poll schedule for outbox consumer

infrastructure_verified.redis: Connected on localhost:6379 (DB 1 for broker, DB 2 for results)
infrastructure_verified.celery_worker: Running (supervisor) - concurrency 2, queues: default, notification_queue, reports
infrastructure_verified.celery_beat: Running (supervisor) - 5-second poll schedule for outbox consumer
